title: Liquidity: Current vs Acid-Test
event: Reyes Co.'s current assets total $150,000: cash $20,000, short-term investments $10,000, accounts receivable $40,000, inventory $70,000, and prepaid expenses $10,000. Current liabilities are $75,000. Compute the current ratio and the acid-test ratio.

computationPaths.narration: The acid test strips out the slow assets — inventory has to be sold and prepaids can never be spent.

memorize.body: Acid-test = (Cash + ST investments + Receivables) ÷ Current liabilities. Inventory and prepaids are OUT.
When the two ratios diverge sharply, inventory is doing the heavy lifting — a liquidity red flag examiners love.

questions.prompt: Using the facts above, the acid-test ratio = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the CURRENT ratio. The acid test excludes inventory and prepaids: 70,000 ÷ 75,000 = 0.93.
You excluded inventory but kept prepaids. Prepaids can't pay bills either — quick assets are cash, investments, receivables.
